isolation.testingTool: Direct shell commands (cargo test, ls, grep)

VAL-VARFONT-001.title: Variable font file loading
VAL-VARFONT-001.evidence: 7 unit tests pass in shaping.rs: varfont_nunito_sans_has_four_axes, varfont_nunito_sans_opsz_axis, varfont_nunito_sans_wdth_axis, varfont_nunito_sans_wght_axis, varfont_nunito_sans_ytlc_axis, varfont_nunito_sans_axis_tags_exact, varfont_nunito_sans_variable_shapes_text. Font file exists: nunito-sans-variable.ttf (569372 bytes).
VAL-VARFONT-001.details: Variable Nunito Sans loads successfully. All four axes (opsz, wght, wdth, YTLC) are detected with correct tag names. Tests assert 4 axes with correct tags and non-degenerate (min != max) ranges. All 7 tests pass.

VAL-VARFONT-002.title: Axis value affects glyph outlines
VAL-VARFONT-002.evidence: 3 unit tests pass: varfont_wght_400_vs_700_different_coverage (asserts different total coverage at wght=400 vs wght=700), varfont_out_of_range_wght_clamped_no_panic (asserts no panic at out-of-range axis value), varfont_wght_2000_equals_max (asserts wght=2000 is clamped to font's declared max range).
VAL-VARFONT-002.details: Setting wght=700 produces measurably different glyph coverage than default weight. Out-of-range axis value wght=2000 is clamped to the font's declared range without panic. All 3 tests pass.

VAL-VARFONT-003.title: Glyph interpolation at arbitrary axis values
VAL-VARFONT-003.evidence: 1 unit test passes: varfont_wght_550_differs_from_400_and_700. Test rasterizes at 3 weights (400, 550, 700) and asserts coverage at 550 differs from both 400 and 700 by >5%, confirming interpolation rather than snapping.
VAL-VARFONT-003.details: Glyph outlines at intermediate axis value wght=550 produce coverage meaningfully distinct from both nearest masters (wght=400 and wght=700). Test confirms >5% pixel difference, proving interpolation.

VAL-VARFONT-004.title: Variable Source Code Pro
VAL-VARFONT-004.evidence: 3 unit tests pass: varfont_source_code_pro_has_wght_axis, varfont_source_code_pro_wght_range_valid, varfont_source_code_pro_variable_shapes_text. Font file exists: source-code-pro-variable.ttf (212692 bytes).
VAL-VARFONT-004.details: Variable Source Code Pro loads with the weight axis detected and valid range. All 3 tests pass.

VAL-CACHE-005.title: Variable font axis values in cache key
VAL-CACHE-005.evidence: 4 unit tests pass: varfont_cache_axis_values_separate_entries (verifies same glyph at wght=400 and wght=700 cached as separate entries with different coverage), varfont_cache_no_axes_vs_with_axes (verifies default vs explicit axes produce separate cache entries), scene_text_run_axis_hash_round_trip (axis hash preserved in scene graph), fallback_font_identifier_hash_includes_axis_values (font identifier hash includes axis values).
VAL-CACHE-005.details: Cache distinguishes glyphs at different axis values. Glyph ID X at wght=400 and glyph ID X at wght=700 are cached as separate entries with different coverage data. Scene graph and fallback identifier hashing also include axis values. All 4 tests pass.

VAL-CROSS-002.title: Variable font axis end-to-end
VAL-CROSS-002.evidence: 1 unit test passes: varfont_wght_700_heavier_than_400. Asserts coverage_sum(wght=700) > coverage_sum(wght=400) for the same glyph, confirming heavier weight produces visibly more ink.
VAL-CROSS-002.details: A variable font axis value (wght=700) produces measurably heavier text than wght=400. The complementary unit test asserts sum of coverage values at wght=700 > sum at wght=400 for the same glyph. Test passes.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: all 6 passed. 21 varfont-prefixed tests + 2 supplementary axis-hash tests all pass (23 total). Font files present and correctly sized. Variable font loading, axis detection, interpolation, caching, and end-to-end coverage all verified.
